properties.description: ATI related instructions or guidance for new students and faculty
properties.title: Guidance for New Students and Faculty

properties.description: This course, while primarily intended for those in the classroom environment, is useful for any employee who serves students with disabilities. Disability is part of the human condition. To ensure inclusion in higher education, campus leaders, faculty, and staff must consider how to fully embrace all students with and without disabilities. When developing a culture of inclusion, colleges and universities have specific responsibilities to students with disabilities to ensure they can learn and achieve their goals.
properties.name: CSU-Serving Students with Disabilities: Introduction

properties.description: Guidance for accessiblity in marketing materials like PDFs, Emails and Flyers
properties.title: Guidance for Accessible Marketing Materials

properties.description: Departments on campus often use marketing tools such as Constant Contact, MailChimp, VerticalResponse, and others to communicate with their target populations. While these tools are a great method of reaching your audience, it is important to ensure that the emails sent out are accessible to people with disabilities.
properties.name: Accessibility of Mass Marketing Emails

Web Accessibility Process for New Websites

properties.content: The web v1 process document does not specifically call out programming techniques for improved accessibility.
properties.name: Missing process related to web programming

properties.content:
Manual Testing Process:

    The DPRC (Disability Programs and Resource Center) uses the Website Manual Evaluation Summary (WMES) template to track accessibility for selected pages on a website or web application.
    Departments are recommended to manually test five pages that best represent the content of the site. Additional pages may be selected by the DPRC for further verification​

 
Scope of Manual Testing:

    Manual testing complements automated testing by addressing accessibility standards that cannot be covered by automated tools. It is a critical part of ensuring the overall accessibility of web content​

    
Assistance for Departments:

    Campus departments are encouraged to perform manual testing with assistance from the DPRC. Training and resources for manual evaluations are also provided as part of the accessibility process​
properties.name: Summary of process document for 1.6

Web Accessibility Process for New Websites

properties.content:
All SF State websites and web applications are required to include a link to SF State's Accessibility Statement, affirming the commitment to accessibility and offering feedback options (page 7).

While there is a general mention of feedback mechanisms and reporting via accessibility statements, details on the exact operational workflow for users reporting issues (e.g., timelines for acknowledgment and resolution beyond escalation) could be clearer.

The document demonstrates a clear structure for addressing web accessibility through published statements and issue reporting mechanisms. However:

There is room for improvement in specifying workflows and user-friendly processes.
The feedback loop for informing individuals of resolutions requires more definition.
properties.name: 1.19-web review

properties.content: Accessibility Statement is in the Drupal Template Footer
properties.name: Accessibility Statement is in the Drupal Template Footer

properties.description: about this and that
properties.name: good times
Web Accessibility Process for New Websites

properties.content:
Ongoing Monitoring: The DPRC (Disability Programs and Resource Center) is responsible for establishing scans of campus websites and web applications using an enterprise accessibility tool. These scans are organized by cabinet area or college and made available upon request​


Quarterly Reports: Accessibility reports summarizing issues such as Priority 1 failures and the total number of errors are distributed quarterly to relevant administrators and staff​


Remediation and Repair Prioritization: Departments are responsible for addressing the identified issues based on established prioritization guidelines, such as the severity and frequency of use of the affected content​


Use of Accessibility Standards: Automated and manual testing processes, along with assistive technology testing, are outlined to ensure compliance with accessibility standards​
properties.name: Summary of process document for 1.4

properties.content:
ATI Accessibility reviews are
conducted upon request before new sites are published.
High level process in place, however no implementation for
22/23
properties.name: ATI Accessibility reviews are conducted upon request before new sites are published. High level process in place, however no implementation for 22/23
Shawn Hicks

properties.description: Academic Technology’s Syllabus tool allows faculty and academic office coordinators to build and manage class syllabi. The service draws content from campus databases and allows users to create, share, and re-use syllabi in ways that save time and allow for a paperless approach to managing this resource. Users can publish accessible versions in multiple formats including directly into iLearn courses.
properties.title: Accessible Syllabus Tool

The service draws content from campus databases and allows users to create, share, and re-use syllabi in ways that save time and allow for a paperless approach to managing this resource. Users can publish accessible versions in multiple formats including directly into iLearn courses.

properties.description: about this and that
properties.name: good times
Web Accessibility Process for New Websites

properties.content:
Distribution of Results:

    The DPRC summarizes the results of accessibility evaluations in a Website Manual Evaluation Summary report.
    The report is sent to the requesting department via the Campus Ticketing System or email​

Responsibilities of Recipients:

    The department receiving the results is responsible for reviewing and correcting errors in the website or application.
    Once corrections are made, the department can request a re-review by the DPRC​

Collaboration for Remediation:

    The DPRC provides technical assistance and consultation to help departments remediate barriers until all issues are resolved​

Tracking and Escalation:

    If issues cannot be resolved in a timely manner, they are escalated to the ADA/504 Compliance Officer through DPRC’s Accessible Technology Services Program Manager​

properties.content:
The web accessibility process document mentions multimedia content as part of the accessibility evaluation process but lacks a comprehensive, detailed process specifically for conducting manual evaluations on video and audio. Key elements such as specific evaluation techniques, tools, prioritization criteria, and multimedia-specific evaluator training are not fully developed

We currently do not have a defined process.
